observations.recommendation:
1.8 In this regard, we recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to
direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant the following:

a) Municipal Treasurer
a.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No.
1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines.
a.2 Prepare, update and submit the Cashbooks and its pertinent
documents to avoid inconvenience at the time of cash
examination.
a.3 Submit the Bond, Statement of Assets, Liabilities and
Networth (SALN), Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD)
and Report of Accountability for Accountable Form (RAAF).

b) Municipal Accountant
b.1 Abide to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996.
b.2 Prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank
Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the
Subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.

2.7 We recommend the management to exert extra effort to collect the Real
Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Receivables, in
pursuant with Section 57 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 464, otherwise
known as the Real Property Tax Code, Section 4 of the Republic Act No.
5447, otherwise known as An Act creating Special Education Fund and
Section 470 (d) (2) of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the
Local Government Code of 1991.

3.10 We recommend to the Head of the Agency to instruct the personnel
concerned to take appropriate planning on the procurement of office
supplies, Other Supplies and Materials Inventory and Textbooks and
Instructional Materials Inventory so as not to exceed the agency’s two
months requirement and the Inventory Committee to submit the Report of
Physical Count of Inventories-Office Supplies Other Supplies and Materials
Inventory and Textbooks and Instructional Materials Inventory, pursuant to
Section 26 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 11518, otherwise known as the
General appropriations Act of Fiscal Year 2021 and Section 124 of the
Manual of the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

4.10 We recommend to the management the following:

a) Local Chief Executive
a.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking,
in accordance with Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
a.2 To create the Inventory Committee composing of Accounting,
Property Divisions/Units and Internal Audit Service/Unit for
the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account, in accordance with
Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020.

b) Inventory Committee
b.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), in accordance
with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
b.2 To conduct the Physical Inventory taking, pursuant to Section
5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006
32
dated January 31, 2020.
b.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE), in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

c) Property Unit
c.1 To ensure that the recording are updated, pursuant to Section
6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
c.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in
accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No.
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.3 To prepare list of non existing/missing PPEs, in accordance
with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
c.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP), in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit, in accordance with
Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

d) Accounting Unit
d.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found
at station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards based on the List of PPEs found at
Station, in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.2 To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of
PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned
accountable officer, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section
6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
d.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop
from the books of accounts the remaining balance as of
December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA
Circular No. 2020-006.
d.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the
balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers,
pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.

e) Internal Audit Service/Unit
e.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory, in accordance with
Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020.

5.10 We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the above
personnel mentioned below the following:

a) Supply Officer
a.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption of Government Motor Vehicle.
a.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption (MFC).
a.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) and
Monthly Fuel Consumption.

b) Drivers
b.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT)
where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for
the month.
b.3 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.

c) Chief Accountant
c.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the
Drivers to the Auditor.

6.6 We recommend the management to adopt the uniform property
identification system, pursuant to Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

7.4 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
municipal accountant to submit the Peace and Order Fund for our
examination and validation, in accordance with Section 122 of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines.

8.3 Hence, we recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct those
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension within the
ninety (90) days period as prescribed in Section 9.4 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2019.

1. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account of ₱1,376,942.78 and to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and Due to account of ₱70,030.73 to the Home Development Mutual Fund, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997 and Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise known as Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on PAG-IBIG, respectively.
observations.managementAction: 1.Except for the Due to GSIS of ₱1,376,942.78, all other Inter Agency Payables were fully settled.
observations.reason: 1.On-going finalization of Memorandum of Agreement with the GSIS for the settlement of the outstanding balance.

observations.recommendation: 2.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer the following: a. Abide to Section 57 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 464, otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code. b.Conduct tax campaign especially to those real property owners. c.Exert extra effort to collect the real property taxes. d.Recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for the tax legislation on tax incentives for those real property owners who would pay their respective taxes early.
observations.managementAction: 2. Management had collected some of the RPT.
observations.reason: 2. Some of the real property tax payers failed to pay their Real Property Tax (RPT).

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend the management to exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax Receivables, Special Education Receivables and Due from LGUs, in pursuant with Section 57 of the Republic of the Philippines Act (R.A.) No. 464, otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code, Section 4 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 5447, otherwise known as An Act creating a Special Education Fund and Section 470 (d) (2) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code 1991.
observations.managementAction: 3. Management had collected some of the RPT.
observations.reason: 3. Some of the real property tax payers failed to pay their Real Property Tax (RPT).

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following: 4.1 To instruct the officer and other personnel concerned to take appropriate planning on the procurement of Inventory Account as a whole so as not to exceed the agency’s two month requirement. 4.2 To instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories – Other Supplies and Materials Inventory, pursuant to Section 25 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 11465, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of Fiscal Year 2020 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: 4. Implemented
observations.reason: 4. Some actions still on-going; Inventory Committee to submit Report on Physical Count.

observations.recommendation: 5.1 Local Chief Executive (LCE) where 5.1.1 Create the Physical Inventory Taking Committee, in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, consisting of the three (3) parties: 5.1.1.1 Representative of the Local Chief Executive as Chairman 5.1.1.2 General Services Officer 5.1.1.3 Municipal Treasurer 5.1.1.4 Physical Inventory Taking Committee 5.1.1.4.1 Prepare the report and submit the Report on the Physical Count – Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: 5.1.1 Create the Physical Inventory Taking Committee and prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count – Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to reclassify from Other Maintenance and Operating Expense to more specific Accounts, in accordance with Section 111 paragraph (2) of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 6. Reclassification was done by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply the policies and procedures on the Other Special Accounts under the General Fund (OSAGF) to the 20% Development Fund and to the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the documents on infrastructures projects, in accordance with Sections 160, 161 and 163 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 18 paragraph (4) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 7. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of PPAs (RFUSPPAI) and the sufficient supporting documents, in accordance with Section 4 paragraph (6) and 112 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 4.2 of the Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.managementAction: 8. Implemented

observations.recommendation:
92. We commend the Municipal Government of Buldon, Maguindanmao for its effort
in the implementation of the lift of the thirty percent (30%) cap on the amount
appropriated for the quick response fund, in accordance with Section 44 paragraph (dd)
of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 11469, otherwise known as an act declaring the existence
of a national emergency arising from the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) situation
and a national policy in connection therewith, and authorizing the President of the
Republic of the Philippines for a limited period and subject to restrictions to exercise
powers necessary and proper to carry other purposes.

72. We recommend the management to exert extra effort to collect the Real
Property Tax Receivables, Special Education Receivables and Due from LGUs, in
pursuant with Section 57 of the Republic of the Philippines Act (R.A.) No. 464,
otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code, Section 4 of the Republic Act
(R.A.) No. 5447, otherwise known as An Act creating a Special Education Fund and
Section 470 (d) (2) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the
Local Government Code of 1991.

67. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following:
67.1 To instruct the officer and other personnel concerned to take
appropriate planning on the procurement of Inventory Account as a
whole so as not to exceed the agency’s two month requirement.
67.2 To instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the
Report on Physical Count of Inventories – Other Supplies and
Materials Inventory, pursuant to Section 25 of the Republic Act
(R.A.) No. 11465, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act
of Fiscal Year 2020 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government
Units, Volume I, respectively.

79. We recommend to the Official and Personnel concerned to do the following:
79.1 Local Chief Executive (LCE)
79.1.1 Create the Physical Inventory Taking Committee, in
accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 92—386 dated October 20, 1992, consisting
of the three (3) parties:
79.1.1.1 Representative of the Local Chief Executive as
Chairman
79.1.1.2 General Services Officer
79.1.1.3 Municipal Treasurer
79.2 Physical Inventory Taking Committee
79.2.1 Prepare the report and submit the Report on the Physical
Count – Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the
audit team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

78. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to reclassify from Other
Maintenance and Operating Expense to more specific Expenses Accounts, in
accordance with Section 111 paragraph (2) of the Presidential Decree No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

87. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to apply the policies and procedures on the Other Special Accounts
under the General Fund (OSAGF) to the 20% Development Fund and Municipal
Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the documents on
infrastructures projects, in accordance with Sections 160, 161 and 163 of the
Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I
and Section 18 paragraph (4) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

102. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of PPAs
Implementation (RFUSPPAI) and the sufficient supporting documents, in
accordance with Sections 4 paragraph (6) and 112 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.)
No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and
Section 4.2 of the Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

110. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer the following:
110.1 Adhere to Section 16 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise
known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
110.2 A written explanation about the non – inclusion of the Solid Waste
Management in its budget for CY 2020.
110.3 For this CY 2021, we highly recommend that in the preparation of
budget, the Solid Waste Management should definitely be included.

106. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension before they would mature
into disallowances, in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account of ₱1,376,942.78 and to the Home Development Mutual Fund the amount of ₱70,030.73 pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 8291 and Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9679, respectively.

observations.recommendation: 3.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the accountable officer to submit the bond and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Form with Money Value and without Money Value in accordance with Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) observations.recommendation: 4.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer the following: a. Exert extra effort to collect the real property taxes. d. Recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for the tax legislation on tax incentives for those real property owners who would pay their respective taxes early.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Supply Officer/Property Custodian to submit the Report of the Physical Count – Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with Section 124 (2) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) - For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: 5. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant the following: 6.1 Municipal Treasurer 6.1.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines. 6.1.2 Prepare and update the Cashbooks and its pertinent documents to avoid inconvenience at the time of cash examination. 6.2 Municipal Accountant 6.2.1 Adhere to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. 6.2.2 Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS). 6.2.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: 6. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant the following: 7.1 Abide to Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAs) for Local Government Units, Volume I. 7.2 Create the Physical Inventory Taking Committee. 7.3 Prepare the report and submit the Report on the Physical Count – Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: 7. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Chief of the Supply Unit to submit to the audit team the Report of Physical Count – Office Supply and Report of Physical Count – Other Supplies and Materials, in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: 8. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension before they would mature into disallowances, in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: 9. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommend the management for the implementation of at least five percent (5%) from the total appropriation for the Gender and Development (GAD) for the year, pursuant to Section 37 (A./.C) of the Act (R.A.) No. 9710, otherwise known as the Magna Carta of Women of 2009 and Section 32 of the General Appropriation Act, Fiscal year 2019.
observations.managementAction: 10. Implemented

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend to the Municipal Government of Buldon, Maguindanao for the submission of the Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: 3. Preparation of budget for CY 2019 was on CY 2018. The Solid Waste Management Plan was completed on March 2019 and was submitted to us.
observations.reason: During the Exit Conference on May 27, 2020, the management informed the action taken. We found it completed on justifiable, so we considered its implementation.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the accountable officer to submit the bond and Statement of Accountability for the Accountable Form with Money Value and Without Money Value in accordance with Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: 3.Accountable Officer could not be contacted.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Supply Officer/Property Custodian to submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with Section 124 (2) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) - For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason: 5. Still in the process of reconciling the PPE.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

57. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant the following:

57.1 Municipal Treasurer
57.1.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.
57.1.2 Prepare and update the Cashbooks and its pertinent
documents to avoid inconvenience at the time of cash
examination.
57.2 Municipal Accountant
57.2.1 Adhere to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996.
57.2.2 Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
57.2.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank
Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the
subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.

Recommendation

63. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant the following:

63.1 Abide to Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-
386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAs) for Local Government
Units, Volume I.
63.2 Create the Physical Inventory Taking Committee.
63.3 Prepare the report and submit the Report on the Physical
Count-Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

We recommend the management to exert extra effort to collect the Real
Property Tax Receivables, Special Education Receivables and Due from LGUs, in
pursuant with Section 57 of the Republic of the Philippines Act (R.A.) No. 464,
otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code, Section 4 of the Republic Act
(R.A.) No. 5447, otherwise known as An Act creating a Special Education Fund and
Section 470 (d) (2) of the Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, otherwise known as the
Local Government Code of 1991.

Recommendation

78. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Chief of the
Supply Unit to submit to the audit team the Report of Physical Count – Office
Supply and Report of Physical Count – Other Supplies and Materials, in accordance
with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992.

Recommendation

84. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension before they would mature
into disallowances, in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

93. We recommend the management for the implementation of at least five
percent (5%) from the total appropriation for the Gender and Development (GAD)
for the year, pursuant to Section 37 (A./.C) of the Act (R.A.) No. 9710, otherwise
known Magna Carta of Women of 2009 and Section 32 of the General
Appropriation Act, Fiscal year 2019.

Recommendation

98. We recommend to the Municipal Government of Buldon, Maguindanao
for the submission of the Social Waste Management expenditures for Calendar Year
2019, in accordance with Section 16 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise
known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

1. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account of ₱1,376,942.78 and to the Government Service and Insurance System (GSIS) and Due to PAGIBIG account of ₱70,030.73 to the Home Development Mutual Fund, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997 and Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise known as Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on PAG-IBIG, respectively.
observations.managementAction: 1.Except for the finalization of Memorandum of Agreement with the GSIS for the settlement of the outstanding balance. all other Inter Agency Payables were fully settled.
observations.reason: 1.On-going

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to submit the Local Government Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 2. In the exit conference the management explained that a resolution was already passed in Sangguiniang Bayan of the Municipality of Buldon, maguindanao with regard to the Local Government Solid Waste Management. Eventually, the management would be preparing the Solid Waste Management Plan. Upon its completion, they would be providing the team a copy in relation to our recommendation.
observations.reason:
2.On-going
2. Still in the process of preparation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Supply Officer to submit to the audit team the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)-Office Supplies, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System For Local Government Units, Volume I.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer the following: a. Abide to Section 57 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 464, otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code. b.Conduct tax campaign especially to those real property owners. c.Exert extra effort to collect the real property taxes. d.Recommend to the Sanggunian Bayan for the tax legislation on tax incentives for those real property owners who would pay their respective taxes early.
observations.reason: 4.Some of the real property tax payers failed to pay their Real Property Tax.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer the following: a. Municipal Accountant submit the Report of the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in violation to Section 114 (3) (4) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting Systems (MNGAS)-For Local Government Units, Volume I. 2. Reconcile the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card with the Stock of the General Service Office. b. Municipal Supply Officer 1. Adhere to Section 124 (2) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting Systems (MNGAS)-For Local Government Units, Volume I. 2. Reconcile the Stock Card (SC) with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC). 3.Prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension and Notice of Disallowance in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

8. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to allocate the amount not less than five percent (5%) from the total budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in the ensuing year to avoid similar incident of CY 2017, in accordance with Section 21 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, otherwise known as an Act Strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Framework and institutionalizing the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan, appropriating funds thereof and other purposes and Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular NO. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

9. We recommend to the management to fast track in the preparation of the Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
10.LCE had already instructed the Municipal Accountant to transfer the ₱2,008.56 from the General Fund to the Special Education Fund.
